title: eremostachys molucelloides var. macrophylla
intro: Eremostachys molucelloides var. macrophylla, also known as the Tibetan wormwood or wormwood of the Himalayas, is a plant species from the Lamiaceae family, commonly found in the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas and Tibetan Plateau. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat gastrointestinal issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in essential oils, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its numerous health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Traditionally, Eremostachys molucelloides var. macrophylla is used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, to treat various ailments, and its essential oils are also used in aromatherapy.
